sourceDocuments: Agriveda Weed & Abiotic Stress JSON (Pages A-D)

prevention: Clean/certified seed free of weed seed
Well-levelled field + good puddling smothers weeds
monitoring: Scout weed flush at 15 & 30 DAT; identify grass vs sedge vs BLW to pick herbicide
cultural: Maintain 3-5 cm standing water (biggest suppressor in transplanted rice)
Rotary/cono-weeder at 20 & 40 DAT in wider spacing

chemical.note: Apply on thin water film; standing rice
The workhorse post-em; drain then re-flood after 2 days

abioticStress.symptoms: Bronze/rusty spots on lower leaves 2-3 wk after transplant, stunting, delayed tillering
abioticStress.cause: Low available Zn, high-pH/calcareous & continuously flooded soils

prevention: Soil apply Zinc sulphate 10 kg/acre basal in Zn-deficient soils
monitoring: Watch bronzing on lower leaves 15-20 DAT

chemical.dose: 1 kg ZnSO4 + 0.5 kg lime in 200 L/acre
chemical.note: Neutralise with lime to avoid leaf scorch

abioticStress.symptoms: Interveinal yellowing of young leaves, whitening in severe
abioticStress.cause: Aerobic soil, high pH, DSR condition

abioticStress.symptoms: Complete submergence, yellowing, plant death if prolonged
abioticStress.cause: Flash flooding, poor drainage

monitoring: Watch water level in flood-prone fields
cultural: Drain excess water fast; avoid N just before flood

chemical.note: Top-dress N after water recedes to aid recovery

abioticStress.symptoms: Leaf-tip burn, poor tillering, patchy stunted growth on salt-affected patches
abioticStress.cause: Saline/sodic soils, poor-quality irrigation water

cultural: Leaching with good water; gypsum + green manure; raised-bed transplanting

abioticStress.symptoms: Crop falls over at grain filling, grain loss, harvest difficulty
abioticStress.cause: Excess N, dense crop, tall varieties, wind/rain

prevention: Semi-dwarf varieties; balanced N (split); avoid excess N
cultural: Proper spacing; drain before heavy wind events; silicon/K nutrition strengthens stem

prevention: Clean seed; proper plant population for canopy shading
monitoring: Scout flush at 15 & 30 DAS; classify grass/sedge/BLW
cultural: Inter-row hoeing/earthing-up at 30-35 DAS (dual: weeds + fall-armyworm-free soil in whorl)

chemical.note: Do NOT use in maize+pulse/veg intercrop; moist soil
Avoid drift to nearby broadleaf crops

abioticStress.symptoms: V-shaped yellowing from tip of lower/older leaves along midrib
abioticStress.cause: Low N, leaching after heavy rain, waterlogging

abioticStress.symptoms: Broad white/yellow interveinal band on young leaves (white bud), stunting
abioticStress.cause: High-pH soils, high P, sandy soils

prevention: Basal Zinc sulphate 10 kg/acre in deficient soils

abioticStress.symptoms: Yellowing, purpling, stunting, root death in low spots after rain
abioticStress.cause: Poor drainage, heavy rain (maize very sensitive)

abioticStress.symptoms: Poor cob fill, tip barrenness, kernel abortion
abioticStress.cause: Drought at flowering/silking (most sensitive stage)

prevention: Ensure irrigation at knee-high, tasseling, silking, grain-fill
Clean seed; adequate plant stand for canopy cover
monitoring: Scout Trianthema flush after first rains
cultural: 1-2 inter-row hoeings at 20 & 35 DAS (usually most economical for this fast dryland crop)

abioticStress.symptoms: Leaf rolling, wilting, poor tillering & grain fill (though bajra is drought-hardy)
abioticStress.cause: Erratic monsoon, sandy low-retention soils

prevention: Drought-tolerant hybrids; sow with monsoon onset
monitoring: Watch leaf rolling in dry spells
cultural: Mulching; conserve moisture; thin to optimal stand

abioticStress.symptoms: Interveinal yellowing of young leaves on calcareous/sandy soils
abioticStress.cause: High-pH light soils, low available Fe

abioticStress.symptoms: Older-leaf yellowing, thin stand, poor tillering
abioticStress.cause: Low N, leaching, sandy soils

abioticStress.symptoms: Poor grain set, chaffy earheads
abioticStress.cause: >42C during flowering

prevention: Adjust sowing so flowering avoids peak heat
Clean seed; optimal plant population for early canopy cover
monitoring: Scout flush at 15 & 30 DAS; classify grass/sedge/BLW

chemical.note: Tank-mix partner where BLW handled separately

abioticStress.symptoms: Interveinal yellowing of young leaves, whitening in severe
abioticStress.cause: High-pH/calcareous soils, waterlogging

abioticStress.symptoms: Uniform yellowing of YOUNG leaves (unlike N which hits old leaves), thin stems
abioticStress.cause: S-deficient soils, no S in fertiliser

prevention: Apply Gypsum/SSP or elemental S basal (oilseed needs S)
cultural: Use SSP instead of DAP to add S

abioticStress.symptoms: Yellowing, wilting, root rot, stand loss in low spots
abioticStress.cause: Heavy rain, poor drainage (soybean sensitive)

abioticStress.symptoms: Excessive shedding of flowers/young pods
abioticStress.cause: Moisture/heat stress, poor nutrition at flowering

weeds.criticalPeriod: First 20-45 DAS; keep weed-free at pegging (40-60 DAS)

cultural: Inter-row hoeing at 20 & 40 DAS BUT stop soil disturbance after pegging (damages pegs)

abioticStress.symptoms: Empty pods (pops), dark plumule, poor kernel fill
abioticStress.cause: Low Ca in pegging zone, sandy soils

prevention: Apply Gypsum at flowering/pegging (the classic groundnut practice)
cultural: Earthing-up to bring Ca to pegging zone

abioticStress.symptoms: Interveinal yellowing of young leaves on calcareous soils
abioticStress.cause: High-pH/calcareous soils

abioticStress.symptoms: Reduced pegging, poor pod fill, yield loss
abioticStress.cause: Dry spell at critical pegging/pod-development stages

abioticStress.symptoms: Aspergillus contamination of kernels (post-harvest safety, drought-linked)
abioticStress.cause: End-season drought + delayed harvest + poor drying

prevention: Avoid end-season moisture stress; timely harvest
Dry kernels to <8% moisture before storage
cultural: Proper curing/drying; avoid mechanical damage; hygienic storage

weeds.criticalPeriod: First 20-40 DAS (short crop, early weed-free vital)

prevention: Clean seed; dense stand for quick canopy
cultural: 1-2 hand weedings at 20 & 40 DAS (often most economical for short Zaid/kharif crop)

abioticStress.symptoms: Yellowing, wilting, root rot, stand loss
abioticStress.cause: Poor drainage, heavy rain (moong very sensitive)

abioticStress.symptoms: S: young-leaf yellowing; P: dull dark-green/purpling, poor nodulation
abioticStress.cause: Low S/P soils, no biofertiliser

prevention: SSP basal (adds P+S); Rhizobium+PSB seed treatment

abioticStress.symptoms: Heavy shedding of flowers/young pods
abioticStress.cause: Heat/moisture stress at flowering

abioticStress.symptoms: Flower drop, poor pod set in peak summer
abioticStress.cause: >40C at flowering in summer moong

prevention: Time sowing so flowering avoids peak heat; heat-tolerant varieties

weeds.criticalPeriod: First 45-60 DAS (slow early growth = long weedy window)

cultural: 2 inter-row hoeings at 25 & 50 DAS; intercropping (with short pulses) smothers weeds & uses early wide gaps

abioticStress.symptoms: Yellowing, wilting, root death, collapse in low spots
abioticStress.cause: Poor drainage, heavy rain (arhar highly waterlogging-sensitive)

prevention: Ridge/broad-bed planting; drainage channels; avoid low-lying fields
cultural: Drain fast; ridge planting is key

abioticStress.symptoms: Heavy shedding of flowers/young pods (arhar naturally sheds a lot)
abioticStress.cause: Moisture stress, heat, poor nutrition, cloudy weather at flowering

abioticStress.symptoms: Fe: young-leaf interveinal chlorosis; Zn: small leaves, interveinal yellowing
abioticStress.cause: High-pH soils, low micronutrients

abioticStress.symptoms: Flower/pod damage, blackening in cold snaps
abioticStress.cause: Frost during winter flowering of long-duration arhar

prevention: Choose duration so flowering avoids frost window
cultural: Light evening irrigation before frost night; smoke

weeds.criticalPeriod: First 60-120 DAP (wide spacing + slow start = heavy prolonged pressure)

cultural: Inter-cultivation + earthing-up; trash mulching between rows smothers weeds & conserves moisture

abioticStress.symptoms: Yellowing, stunted growth, reduced tillering, root death; worsens red rot
abioticStress.cause: Poor drainage, prolonged flooding

prevention: Field drainage; ridge-furrow; avoid low spots
cultural: Drain excess; trench drainage in waterlogged belts

abioticStress.symptoms: Leaf rolling, dried tips, poor internode elongation, low tonnage & sugar
abioticStress.cause: Dry spells in formative/grand-growth phase

prevention: Drip irrigation (best for cane); ensure formative-phase irrigation
cultural: Trash mulch conserves moisture; drip; skip-furrow in scarcity

abioticStress.symptoms: Interveinal yellowing/whitening of young/top leaves
abioticStress.cause: Calcareous/high-pH soils, waterlogging

abioticStress.symptoms: Canes fall over in grand-growth/mature phase, sugar loss, harvest difficulty
abioticStress.cause: Excess N, tall dense crop, wind, waterlogging

cultural: Earthing-up; trash-twist propping/tying of canes; detrashing

abioticStress.symptoms: Gaps, low tiller survival, uneven stand
abioticStress.cause: Poor sett quality, moisture stress, temperature extremes at planting

prevention: Quality 3-budded setts; sett treatment; optimal planting temp/moisture
cultural: Gap-filling; light irrigation post-planting; earthing-up to promote tillering
Black plastic mulch (controls weeds + conserves moisture)

chemical.note: Apply before transplanting on moist soil

abioticStress.symptoms: Excessive flower drop, few fruits
abioticStress.cause: Heat/cold extremes, moisture stress, low B, poor pollination

prevention: Consistent irrigation; balanced nutrition incl. boron

abioticStress.symptoms: Marginal leaf scorching/browning on older leaves, poor fruit quality
abioticStress.cause: Low K, sandy soils, heavy fruiting drain

abioticStress.symptoms: Yellowing, wilting, root rot, worsens bacterial wilt
abioticStress.cause: Poor drainage

abioticStress.symptoms: Pale scalded patches / cracks on exposed fruit
abioticStress.cause: Intense sun on exposed fruit, irregular irrigation

cultural: Avoid over-pruning; mulch for steady moisture; timely harvest
Black plastic/straw mulch; inter-row hoeing at 30 & 55 DAT

abioticStress.symptoms: Heavy shedding of flowers/buds, low fruit set
abioticStress.cause: Heat >35C, moisture stress, low boron, N excess

abioticStress.symptoms: Tip die-back, general decline (distinguish from anthracnose)
abioticStress.cause: K/Ca deficiency + stress (often confused with disease)

monitoring: Rule out anthracnose (acervuli) before treating as nutrient

abioticStress.symptoms: Bleached leathery patches on exposed fruit
abioticStress.cause: Intense sun, sparse canopy

abioticStress.symptoms: Interveinal yellowing of young leaves
abioticStress.cause: High-pH soils, waterlogging

keyWeeds: Open field: Echinochloa, Cyperus, Amaranthus, Commelina

prevention: Black plastic mulch (standard in capsicum, near-total weed control)
cultural: Mulch + manual removal in holes/aisles; no herbicide needed under polyhouse

abioticStress.symptoms: Sunken dark leathery rot at blossom end of fruit (NOT a disease)
abioticStress.cause: Calcium deficiency + irregular irrigation/humidity swings (common in polyhouse)

prevention: Steady irrigation (drip + fertigation); adequate Ca; avoid moisture swings & excess N/K

abioticStress.symptoms: Shedding of flowers/young fruit
abioticStress.cause: Temp extremes (>32C day / cold nights), low light, humidity swings, poor pollination in polyhouse

cultural: Shade-net in summer; pollination aid (air blower/gentle shake)

abioticStress.symptoms: Bleached scalded patches on exposed fruit, wilting midday
abioticStress.cause: High light/heat, sparse canopy, poor cooling

abioticStress.symptoms: Fe: young-leaf interveinal chlorosis; Mg: older-leaf interveinal yellowing
abioticStress.cause: Imbalanced fertigation, high-pH media/water

prevention: Balanced water-soluble fertigation; monitor media EC/pH

cultural: Inter-row hoeing + earthing-up at 25 & 45 DAT; mulching

chemical.note: Moist soil; do not spray on foliage

abioticStress.symptoms: Strap-like/ribbon narrow distorted leaves, poor/no curd; interveinal chlorosis first
abioticStress.cause: Mo deficiency, acidic soils (low pH)

prevention: Lime acidic soils to pH 6.5; adequate Mo
monitoring: Watch strappy new leaves in nursery/early field
cultural: Liming; treat nursery beds with Mo

abioticStress.symptoms: Brown/rusty water-soaked patches on curd, hollow/cracked stem, bitter curd
abioticStress.cause: B deficiency, light/high-pH soils, dry spells

prevention: Basal Borax 4-5 kg/acre in B-deficient soils
monitoring: Inspect curd & split stem at curding
cultural: Maintain even moisture (B uptake drops in dry soil)

abioticStress.symptoms: Small useless curds form too early on undersized plants
abioticStress.cause: Wrong maturity-group variety for season, N deficiency, cold/stress at seedling

prevention: Pick correct early/mid/late variety for the sowing window; healthy N nutrition
monitoring: Watch early curd on small plants
cultural: Avoid transplant shock/stress; adequate N early

chemical.input: 2% urea foliar to push vegetative growth

abioticStress.symptoms: Curd surface becomes loose, ricey, fuzzy (bracts/flower stalks show)
abioticStress.cause: High temperature during curd development, delayed harvest

monitoring: Watch curd texture in warm spells
cultural: Harvest promptly at compact stage; shade in heat
Inter-row hoeing + earthing-up at 25 & 45 DAT; mulching

abioticStress.symptoms: Mature heads split open, quality & market loss
abioticStress.cause: Sudden heavy irrigation/rain after dry spell (rapid water uptake) at maturity, delayed harvest

prevention: Even irrigation; avoid heavy irrigation after dry spell near maturity
cultural: Harvest promptly at firm stage; root-pruning (twist plant) slows uptake to delay split

abioticStress.symptoms: Brown scorched margins of inner wrapper leaves inside head
abioticStress.cause: Ca deficiency + rapid growth/moisture swings + high N/K

prevention: Steady irrigation; adequate Ca; avoid excess N
monitoring: Cut sample heads to inspect inner leaf margins

abioticStress.symptoms: Plant sends up flower stalk without forming proper head
abioticStress.cause: Prolonged low temperature (vernalisation) on over-mature transplants

prevention: Right variety for season; correct transplant age; avoid too-early cold-season sowing
cultural: Use young healthy transplants; season-appropriate variety

abioticStress.symptoms: B: hollow/cracked stem, corky head; Mg: interveinal yellowing of older leaves
abioticStress.cause: Light/high-pH soils, heavy leaching

prevention: Clean seed; plastic mulch on beds
cultural: Black plastic mulch (best: weeds + moisture + clean fruit + less fruit rot); trellising; hand weeding in furrows

abioticStress.symptoms: Flower drop, curved/crooked/nubby fruit
abioticStress.cause: Poor pollination (low bee activity/polyhouse), temp extremes, K/B deficiency, moisture stress

prevention: Ensure pollinators (open field); gynoecious hybrids need pollinizer or parthenocarpic types in polyhouse
cultural: Introduce bees; even irrigation; balanced K/B

abioticStress.symptoms: Bitter fruit (cucurbitacin buildup)
abioticStress.cause: Moisture/temperature stress, wide swings, low fertility

prevention: Steady irrigation; avoid stress; balanced nutrition

abioticStress.symptoms: Yellowing, wilting, root rot, vine collapse
abioticStress.cause: Poor drainage, heavy rain

prevention: Raised beds + drainage; plastic mulch sheds water

abioticStress.symptoms: N: older-leaf yellowing, pale vines; Mg: interveinal yellowing older leaves
abioticStress.cause: Leaching (sandy soils), heavy fruiting drain

prevention: Split N via fertigation; adequate Mg

cultural: 1-2 inter-row hoeings at 20 & 40 DAS; mulching; keep weed-free first 40-45 days

abioticStress.symptoms: Shedding of buds/flowers, poor pod set
abioticStress.cause: Heat >40C, moisture stress, N excess, low boron

abioticStress.symptoms: Pods turn woody, fibrous, unmarketable
abioticStress.cause: Delayed/infrequent picking, moisture stress, over-maturity

prevention: Pick every 2-3 days at tender stage; steady irrigation
cultural: Frequent harvest; mulch for even moisture

abioticStress.symptoms: Fe: young-leaf interveinal chlorosis; Zn: small leaves, interveinal yellowing, stunting
abioticStress.cause: High-pH/sandy soils, heavy cropping

abioticStress.symptoms: Yellowing, wilting, stunting, root rot
abioticStress.cause: Poor drainage, heavy rain
